methods.description: Process for examining skeletal collections to identify and select specimens exhibiting complete bilateral or unilateral Dived Zygoma (DZ), while excluding partial sutures and trauma.

steps.description: Human skeletons from prehistoric and historic periods stored at the Research Center for Chinese Frontier Archeology at Jilin University were examined for signs of bilateral or unilateral DZ.
steps.searchable_summary: Visually examine skeletal remains for specific anatomical variations (Dived Zygoma).
inputs: Human skeletal collections (from 49 archeological sites in Northern China)

steps.description: complete division of the zygomatic bone conditions, while partial supernumerary sutures were excluded.
steps.searchable_summary: Filter identified specimens to exclude those with partial sutures.
outputs: Specimens with complete DZ or other features

steps.description: all supernumerary sutures on the identified DZ specimens were further differentiated from specimens with unhealed trauma fractures or other external force fractures.
steps.searchable_summary: Differentiate identified specimens to exclude those with trauma fractures.
inputs: Identified DZ specimens (after partial suture exclusion)
outputs: Confirmed DZ specimens for further analysis

methods.description: Acquisition of Cone Beam Computed Tomography (CBCT) scans for identified Dived Zygoma (DZ) specimens.

steps.description: The identified specimens were also examined using a K9500 CBCT scanner (Kodak Dental Systems, Carestream Health Inc. Rochester, New York)
steps.searchable_summary: Acquire CBCT scans of selected skeletal specimens.
outputs: CBCT scan data (DICOM or similar format)

steps.description: within parameters of 80 kV; 80 mA; slice thickness of 0.3 mm in increments of 0.2 mm.
steps.searchable_summary: Configure CBCT scanner settings for image acquisition.
inputs: scanning parameters (80 kV, 80 mA, 0.3 mm slice thickness, 0.2 mm increments)

methods.description: Calculation of the prevalence of complete horizontal division of the zygoma.

steps.description: prevalence pertaining to the complete horizontal division of the zygoma by the individual ... were calculated separately.
steps.searchable_summary: Calculate the percentage of individuals exhibiting Dived Zygoma.

steps.description: prevalence pertaining to the complete horizontal division of the zygoma ... and side were calculated separately.
steps.searchable_summary: Calculate the percentage of sides exhibiting Dived Zygoma.
inputs: Total number of sides examined (2 * total individuals)

methods.description: Recording external morphological features and taking specific measurements of the zygomatic bone and related craniofacial structures.

steps.description: The morphology of the craniofacial bones was also recorded by examining the size, shape, and special features of the zygomatic bone, including its height, breadth, area, and symmetry.
steps.searchable_summary: Visually record morphological characteristics of craniofacial bones.

steps.description: in order to quantify the morphological impacts incurred by the DZ phenomenon, we selected and designed several cranial measurements based on zygoma shape, incorporating upper facial height (n-pr), height and breadth of the zygoma, minimum breadth of the zygomatic body, the height and thickness of the zygomatic arch, thickness regarding the zygomatic process of the maxilla, and the breadth of the zygomatic process of the frontal bone ( Figure 2).
steps.searchable_summary: Define specific anthropometric measurements for craniofacial analysis.

steps.description: The measurements were taken on both sides
steps.searchable_summary: Perform anthropometric measurements on skeletal specimens.
outputs: Raw measurement values for each side

steps.description: side-based comparisons were carried out in unilateral DZ specimens to examine the asymmetry between the DZ side and the normal side by calculating relevant size ratios.
steps.searchable_summary: Calculate ratios or differences between measurements on affected and unaffected sides in unilateral cases.
inputs: Raw measurement values for unilateral DZ specimens (DZ side, normal side)

steps.description: in bilateral test subjects, the averages of ... measurements on both sides were calculated.
steps.searchable_summary: Calculate the average of measurements from both sides in bilateral cases.
inputs: Raw measurement values for bilateral DZ specimens (left side, right side)

steps.description: 42 adult individuals with normal zygoma from the archeological sites were chosen as a control group, with the sex and age numbers of the selected sample similar to that of the DZ samples.
steps.searchable_summary: Select a control group from the skeletal collection matching study group characteristics (sex, age).
inputs: Criteria for control group (normal zygoma, sex, age)

steps.description: all measurements were conducted by the same observer (QZ) three times
steps.searchable_summary: Repeat measurements multiple times by the same observer for reliability.
outputs: Multiple sets of raw measurement values

steps.description: average values were recorded.
steps.searchable_summary: Calculate and record the average of repeated measurements.
inputs: Multiple sets of raw measurement values

methods.description: Assessment of facial asymmetry patterns in skulls with unilateral Dived Zygoma (DZ) using CT images to measure cortical bone thickness.

steps.description: CT images were used to assess facial asymmetry patterns in skulls with unilateral DZ conditions by measuring the thickness of the cortical bone.
steps.searchable_summary: Utilize CT scan data to analyze facial asymmetry in unilateral cases.
inputs: CBCT scan data for unilateral DZ specimens

steps.description: five points located on the body of the zygoma were selected, including the zygomatic process of the maxilla, the frontal zygomatic process, and the zygomatic arch ( Figure 2).
steps.searchable_summary: Identify and mark specific anatomical points on CT images for measurement.

steps.description: determination of cortical bone thicknesses was performed using Mimics Research 16.0 software (Materialise Inc., Leuven, Belgium)
steps.searchable_summary: Measure cortical bone thickness from CT data using specialized software.

steps.description: to create measurements from virtual CT reconstructions (which were derived from the bone scans).
steps.searchable_summary: Generate virtual 3D reconstructions from CT scans for measurement.

steps.description: all measurements were conducted by the same observer (QZ) three times
steps.searchable_summary: Repeat CT-based measurements multiple times by the same observer for reliability.
outputs: Multiple sets of CT-based measurement values

steps.description: average values were recorded.
steps.searchable_summary: Calculate and record the average of repeated CT-based measurements.
inputs: Multiple sets of CT-based measurement values

methods.description: Application of statistical tests to analyze collected data on Dived Zygoma (DZ) prevalence, morphology, and asymmetry.

steps.description: Data were then analyzed using a statistical analysis program named GraphPad 6.0 for Windows (GraphPad Software Inc., La Jolla, California).
steps.searchable_summary: Load and prepare data for statistical analysis in GraphPad software.
inputs: Collected measurement data (prevalence, external measurements, CT measurements)
Group assignments (DZ vs control, unilateral vs bilateral, side)

steps.description: Descriptive statistics ... were used to summarize ...
steps.searchable_summary: Calculate descriptive statistics (e.g., mean, standard deviation, counts) for study variables.

steps.description: ... Student t tests were used to ... assess facial skeletal asymmetry.
steps.searchable_summary: Perform Student's t-tests to compare means between groups or sides.
inputs: Measurement data for groups/sides being compared

steps.description: nonparametric Chi-square row by column tests for independence were used to assess group differences and their association with facial morphologies.
steps.searchable_summary: Perform Chi-square tests to assess association between categorical variables (e.g., group vs. morphology).
inputs: Categorical data (e.g., group membership, morphological classification)

steps.description: The significance level was set at α = 0.05.
steps.searchable_summary: Define the alpha level for statistical significance testing.
